evidence: All API response bodies are constructed as Map<String, Object> throughout ExplorationHandlers, SketchHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, BcqlHandlers, VisualizationHandlers — no typed response DTOs exist, so response shape is not checked at compile time.

SketchHandlers.executeAndFormatCollocations returns null as a sentinel for 'no results found' instead of Optional<ExecutedSketch> — callers must null-check, and the method's return type does not declare this contract.

@Nullable annotations from jspecify are used in key places: CollocateQueryHelper.buildAndRankCollocates posMap parameter, BlackLabSnippetParser return types, ExplorationHandlers.validateExploreRequest, RelationConfig.computeDeprel — good practice for nullable returns.
Optional<RelationType> used in RelationConfig instead of nullable String — preserves type-level intent and forces callers to handle absent case explicitly.
QueryResults record hierarchy (WordSketchResult, CollocateResult, SnippetResult) are typed records — strong typing within the query result domain.
GrammarConfig.getRelation returns Optional<RelationConfig> — type-safe absence handling throughout config lookup.
JSON parsing via fastjson2 JSONObject inherently bypasses static typing; field access via getString/getIntValue uses runtime reflection — unavoidable for dynamic JSON but creates an untyped boundary.
BlackLabQueryExecutor.collectFrequenciesFromGroups uses Function<String, String> lambdas — typed functional interface prevents string/other confusion at call sites.
ConcordanceHandlers: r.rawXml() != null ? r.rawXml() : '' — correctly handles nullable rawXml() return consistent with @Nullable annotation.
RelationConfig is a well-typed record with Optional<RelationType> and PosGroup enum fields — no raw strings for typed domain values.

strengths: Optional<RelationType> and PosGroup enum prevent raw string comparisons for domain types in the config layer.
@Nullable from jspecify is applied consistently to all public methods with nullable returns in the query and API layers.
QueryResults record types (WordSketchResult, CollocateResult) provide strong typing for the data flowing from query layer to API layer.
Optional<RelationConfig> return from GrammarConfig.getRelation forces callers to handle absence explicitly rather than checking for null.
type_safety.issue_character: The systematic weakness is that the type system stops at the API handler boundary — response JSON is assembled as untyped Map<String, Object> maps, and a null sentinel is used as a return value in one internal method rather than Optional.
type_safety.score_rationale: The internal domain model and query layer have good type discipline — records, enums, and Optional are used appropriately. The score is held back by the systematic use of Map<String, Object> for all API response construction, which makes it impossible to detect response shape regressions at compile time, and by the null-as-sentinel pattern in executeAndFormatCollocations. This is a common pattern in Java HTTP server code but represents a real typing gap that affects the whole API layer.

issues.summary: All API responses built as Map<String, Object> with no compile-time shape validation
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.buildBaseExploreResponse: Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>(); response.put("status", "ok"); response.put("relation_type", relationType); — keys are literal strings, values are Object
SketchHandlers.formatWordSketchResult: Map<String, Object> word = new HashMap<>(); word.put("lemma", result.lemma()); — response shape defined only by these string puts
BcqlHandlers.buildBcqlResponse: response.put("total_results", results.size()); — an int put as Object, no type contract
issues.suggestion: Introduce typed response record classes (e.g. SketchResponse, ExploreResponse) and serialize them to JSON via a serialization utility. This captures response contracts in the type system and allows compile-time detection of field name/type regressions.

issues.summary: executeAndFormatCollocations returns null as a sentinel for empty results instead of Optional
evidence: private ExecutedSketch executeAndFormatCollocations(...) throws IOException { ... if (results.isEmpty()) return null; ... }
Callers: 'if (sketch != null) { byRelation.put(rel.id(), builder.apply(rel, sketch)); }' — null check required at call site
Return type is ExecutedSketch not Optional<ExecutedSketch>, so nullability is not expressed in the type
issues.suggestion: Change return type to Optional<ExecutedSketch> and return Optional.empty() for the no-results case. Update all call sites to use ifPresent or map.

root_causes: Java's com.sun.net.httpserver requires manual JSON construction, making typed response DTOs non-trivial to add without a JSON serialization framework already in place (fastjson2 is present but not used for typed serialization).
